item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This works as a concentration (and re-hashing) of the antimemetics milieu - tlaol indeed. A little hard to follow sometimes - I'm not quite sure I understand the reverse aging effect of mnestics, for example. And I didn't feel as though the historical connection was the point of the tale, as much as an excuse to post it in the contest. But there are some good turns of phrase to illustrate the mnestic-high, and I'm definitely up for moving the story forward.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you for sending me back down the antimemetics rabbit hole. This is a very handy collection of skips and stories, this is. :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Antimemetics and mindfuckery. A little hard to read, but I enjoyed it. The idea that the Philadelphia Experiment is some bizarre, memetic-centered form of mind control is rather horrifying; it's one of the better-known conspiracy theories out there. The integration of all these crosslinks are well-done, and they've gotten me interested in antimemetics again.

Overall, +1

item.title: Re: meems
item.content:encoded:
Thoughts
hide

I think this ties into history well enough, I was asking myself the same question until the USS Eldridge came up and I got what the story was going for. I liked a lot of the writing here, I especially liked the last few lines. Very well done. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
For these matters, it's usually best to consult with the organiser.

I WOULD HAVE IF ROGET WASN'T SUCH A BUTT >:C

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I hope that it's a major enough element for this to qualify as a contest entry.

For these matters, it's usually best to consult with the organiser.

Anyway, IMO, it works. I did ask, since I could not find anything meaningful from Googling 'Project Viewpoint'.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Huh. A tale about Foundation history that takes place far in the future, during someone's massive drug trip. Neat way to go about things. As has been pointed out, this is a solid addition to the Anitmemetics lore on the site. While the historical aspect of the tale is subtle, it certainly is there.

+1. Well done Aiden.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The majority of the tale is an infodump about a World War II project that's the source of the Philadelphia Experiment (the USS Eldridge) and the Philadelphia Experiment (the conspiracy theory propagated by Project Viewpoint). Since historical conspiracy theories are both an important plot point and a backdrop to the story here, I hope that it's a major enough element for this to qualify as a contest entry.

ETA: Rereading this, I realise this is probably going to come off as snappy. I'll admit I could do more work with emphasising the historical aspect more. That was pointed out in feedback, but the problem was working it in organically. ^^;

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It is a nice addition to the Antimemetics section of Foundation lore, but seriously, what is the historic context here? For purpose of the contest, I feel it is less salient.

item.title: meems
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to minmin, TyGently, ARD and GreenWolf for reviewing this. It's been a while since we've seen an Antimemetics (or, well, Counterconceptual) tale on the site and I'd like to think a hiscon entry tying the Division together with a couple of other… interesting tidbits of the Foundation set in history would be a good way to revive the canon.

Here's hoping this makes sense to people. And that it qualifies for the contest, I really don't want to have to have a second barely qualifying entry.

ETA: SCP-1345 is one of my favourite SCPs and the skip I'd promote on the long, long, long, long shot that I win this contest. Go check it out.
